_comment: KPI definitions for the Olist e-commerce business. Each KPI includes a business definition, SQL formula, and relevant tables. Used by the agent for precise data analysis.

revenue.label: Total Revenue
revenue.definition: Sum of product price and freight value across all delivered orders. This is the top-line business metric.
revenue.sql_formula: SELECT SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

product_revenue.label: Product Revenue
product_revenue.definition: Revenue from product sales only, excluding freight charges.
product_revenue.sql_formula: SELECT SUM(oi.price) AS product_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

freight_revenue.label: Freight Revenue
freight_revenue.definition: Revenue collected from shipping charges only.
freight_revenue.sql_formula: SELECT SUM(oi.freight_value) AS freight_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

freight_ratio.label: Freight-to-Revenue Ratio
freight_ratio.definition: Percentage of total revenue that comes from freight charges. High values indicate customers are paying a lot for shipping.
freight_ratio.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(oi.freight_value) * 100.0 / SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value), 2) AS freight_ratio_pct FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

monthly_revenue.label: Monthly Revenue
monthly_revenue.definition: Total revenue grouped by month. Used to identify revenue trends over time.
monthly_revenue.sql_formula: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS month, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

revenue_by_category.label: Revenue by Product Category
revenue_by_category.definition: Total revenue broken down by product category (in English). Identifies best-performing product lines.
revenue_by_category.sql_formula: SELECT t.product_category_name_english AS category, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY category ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10

revenue_by_state.label: Revenue by State
revenue_by_state.definition: Total revenue generated by customers from each Brazilian state.
revenue_by_state.sql_formula: SELECT c.customer_state, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY revenue DESC

aov.label: Average Order Value (AOV)
aov.definition: Average revenue generated per order. A key e-commerce health metric.
aov.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) / COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id), 2) AS aov FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

revenue_per_customer.label: Average Revenue per Customer
revenue_per_customer.definition: Total revenue divided by number of unique customers. Measures how much value each customer brings.
revenue_per_customer.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) / COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id), 2) AS revenue_per_customer FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

total_orders.label: Total Orders
total_orders.definition: Total number of orders placed across all statuses.
total_orders.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_orders FROM orders

delivered_orders.label: Delivered Orders
delivered_orders.definition: Number of orders that have been successfully delivered to customers.
delivered_orders.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(*) AS delivered_orders FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered'

order_status_breakdown.label: Order Status Breakdown
order_status_breakdown.definition: Count of orders by each status: delivered, shipped, canceled, processing, invoiced, unavailable, created, approved.
order_status_breakdown.sql_formula: SELECT order_status, COUNT(*) AS count FROM orders GROUP BY order_status ORDER BY count DESC

cancellation_rate.label: Order Cancellation Rate
cancellation_rate.definition: Percentage of all orders that were canceled. High cancellation rates indicate potential issues with stock, pricing, or delivery promises.
cancellation_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN order_status = 'canceled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS cancellation_rate_pct FROM orders

orders_per_month.label: Orders per Month
orders_per_month.definition: Number of orders placed each month. Shows business volume trends over time.
orders_per_month.sql_formula: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_purchase_timestamp) AS month, COUNT(*) AS orders FROM orders GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

avg_items_per_order.label: Average Items per Order
avg_items_per_order.definition: Average number of items included in each order. Higher values suggest customers buy in bulk.
avg_items_per_order.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(*) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT order_id), 2) AS avg_items_per_order FROM order_items

total_customers.label: Total Unique Customers
total_customers.definition: Number of distinct customers who have placed at least one order.
total_customers.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id) AS total_customers FROM customers

customers_by_state.label: Customers by State
customers_by_state.definition: Number of customers from each Brazilian state. Helps identify geographic concentration.
customers_by_state.sql_formula: SELECT customer_state, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id) AS customers FROM customers GROUP BY customer_state ORDER BY customers DESC

customers_by_city.label: Top 10 Customer Cities
customers_by_city.definition: Cities with the most customers. Shows urban concentration of the customer base.
customers_by_city.sql_formula: SELECT customer_city, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_unique_id) AS customers FROM customers GROUP BY customer_city ORDER BY customers DESC LIMIT 10

repeat_purchase_rate.label: Repeat Purchase Rate
repeat_purchase_rate.definition: Percentage of customers who placed more than one order. A higher rate indicates strong customer loyalty.
repeat_purchase_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS repeat_rate_pct FROM (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) sub

new_customers_per_month.label: New Customers per Month
new_customers_per_month.definition: Number of customers making their first-ever purchase each month.
new_customers_per_month.sql_formula: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', first_order) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_customers FROM (SELECT customer_id, MIN(order_purchase_timestamp) AS first_order FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) sub GROUP BY month ORDER BY month

clv.label: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)
clv.definition: Average total revenue generated by a single customer over their entire history with the business.
clv.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(AVG(customer_revenue), 2) AS avg_clv FROM (SELECT o.customer_id, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS customer_revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY o.customer_id) sub

top_customers_by_revenue.label: Top 10 Customers by Revenue
top_customers_by_revenue.definition: Customers who have generated the most revenue. These are the most valuable accounts to protect from churn.
top_customers_by_revenue.sql_formula: SELECT o.customer_id, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS total_spent FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY o.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 10

top_product_categories.label: Top Product Categories by Sales Volume
top_product_categories.definition: Product categories with the highest number of items sold.
top_product_categories.sql_formula: SELECT t.product_category_name_english AS category, COUNT(*) AS items_sold FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name GROUP BY category ORDER BY items_sold DESC LIMIT 10

total_products.label: Total Products in Catalogue
total_products.definition: Total number of distinct products listed by sellers.
total_products.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT product_id) AS total_products FROM products

avg_price_by_category.label: Average Product Price by Category
avg_price_by_category.definition: Average listed price per product category. Helps understand pricing tiers.
avg_price_by_category.sql_formula: SELECT t.product_category_name_english AS category, ROUND(AVG(oi.price), 2) AS avg_price FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name GROUP BY category ORDER BY avg_price DESC

total_sellers.label: Total Active Sellers
total_sellers.definition: Number of distinct sellers who have completed at least one delivered order.
total_sellers.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT oi.seller_id) AS active_sellers FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

top_sellers_by_revenue.label: Top 10 Sellers by Revenue
top_sellers_by_revenue.definition: Sellers who have generated the most revenue across all their orders.
top_sellers_by_revenue.sql_formula: SELECT oi.seller_id, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS total_revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY oi.seller_id ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10

sellers_by_state.label: Sellers by State
sellers_by_state.definition: Geographic distribution of sellers across Brazilian states.
sellers_by_state.sql_formula: SELECT seller_state, COUNT(DISTINCT seller_id) AS seller_count FROM sellers GROUP BY seller_state ORDER BY seller_count DESC

on_time_delivery_rate.label: On-Time Delivery Rate
on_time_delivery_rate.definition: Percentage of delivered orders where actual delivery date was on or before the estimated delivery date.
on_time_delivery_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN order_delivered_customer_date <= order_estimated_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS on_time_rate_pct FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered' AND order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL AND order_estimated_delivery_date IS NOT NULL

late_delivery_rate.label: Late Delivery Rate
late_delivery_rate.definition: Percentage of delivered orders that arrived after the promised delivery date.
late_delivery_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN order_delivered_customer_date > order_estimated_delivery_date THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS late_rate_pct FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered' AND order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL AND order_estimated_delivery_date IS NOT NULL

avg_delivery_time_days.label: Average Actual Delivery Time (Days)
avg_delivery_time_days.definition: Average number of days from order purchase to customer receipt.
avg_delivery_time_days.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(AVG((julianday(order_delivered_customer_date) - julianday(order_purchase_timestamp))), 1) AS avg_delivery_days FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered' AND order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL

avg_estimated_delivery_days.label: Average Estimated Delivery Time (Days)
avg_estimated_delivery_days.definition: Average number of days promised to customers for delivery at time of purchase.
avg_estimated_delivery_days.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(AVG((julianday(order_estimated_delivery_date) - julianday(order_purchase_timestamp))), 1) AS avg_estimated_days FROM orders WHERE order_estimated_delivery_date IS NOT NULL

delivery_time_by_state.label: Average Delivery Time by Customer State
delivery_time_by_state.definition: Average actual delivery days broken down by the customer's state. Useful for identifying geographic delivery bottlenecks.
delivery_time_by_state.sql_formula: SELECT c.customer_state, ROUND(AVG(julianday(o.order_delivered_customer_date) - julianday(o.order_purchase_timestamp)), 1) AS avg_days FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' AND o.order_delivered_customer_date IS NOT NULL GROUP BY c.customer_state ORDER BY avg_days DESC

payment_type_breakdown.label: Orders by Payment Type
payment_type_breakdown.definition: Distribution of payment methods: credit_card, boleto, voucher, debit_card.
payment_type_breakdown.sql_formula: SELECT payment_type, COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS order_count, ROUND(SUM(payment_value), 2) AS total_value FROM order_payments GROUP BY payment_type ORDER BY order_count DESC

avg_installments.label: Average Payment Installments
avg_installments.definition: Average number of installments customers use for credit card payments. High installment usage suggests price sensitivity.
avg_installments.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(AVG(payment_installments), 2) AS avg_installments FROM order_payments WHERE payment_type = 'credit_card' AND payment_installments > 0

high_installment_orders.label: High Installment Orders (>6 installments)
high_installment_orders.definition: Number of orders paid in more than 6 installments. Indicator of customers financing large purchases.
high_installment_orders.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT order_id) AS high_installment_orders FROM order_payments WHERE payment_installments > 6

avg_review_score.label: Average Review Score
avg_review_score.definition: Average customer satisfaction score across all reviewed orders. Scale: 1 (worst) to 5 (best).
avg_review_score.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(AVG(review_score), 2) AS avg_review_score FROM order_reviews

review_score_distribution.label: Review Score Distribution
review_score_distribution.definition: Count of reviews at each score level (1-5). Useful for understanding satisfaction patterns.
review_score_distribution.sql_formula: SELECT review_score, COUNT(*) AS count, ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM order_reviews), 2) AS pct FROM order_reviews GROUP BY review_score ORDER BY review_score

low_review_rate.label: Low Review Rate (Score 1-2)
low_review_rate.definition: Percentage of orders that received a score of 1 or 2. A high rate is a strong signal of poor customer experience.
low_review_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN review_score <= 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS low_score_pct FROM order_reviews

review_rate.label: Review Submission Rate
review_rate.definition: Percentage of delivered orders that received a customer review.
review_rate.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT r.order_id) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id), 2) AS review_rate_pct FROM orders o LEFT JOIN order_reviews r ON o.order_id = r.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered'

review_score_by_category.label: Average Review Score by Product Category
review_score_by_category.definition: Customer satisfaction scores broken down by product category. Identifies which categories have quality or delivery issues.
review_score_by_category.sql_formula: SELECT t.product_category_name_english AS category, ROUND(AVG(r.review_score), 2) AS avg_score, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM order_reviews r JOIN orders o ON r.order_id = o.order_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name GROUP BY category ORDER BY avg_score ASC LIMIT 10

churn_rate_overall.label: Overall Churn Rate
churn_rate_overall.definition: Percentage of customers flagged as churned (no purchase in last 30 days from snapshot 2011-12-10). Derived from the churn model's training features.
churn_rate_overall.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN (julianday('2011-12-10') - julianday(last_order)) > 30 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 2) AS churn_rate_pct FROM (SELECT customer_id, MAX(order_purchase_timestamp) AS last_order FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY customer_id) sub

at_risk_customers.label: At-Risk Customers (Inactive 20-30 days)
at_risk_customers.definition: Customers who have not purchased in 20-30 days from the snapshot date — approaching churn but not yet churned. High-priority for re-engagement campaigns.
at_risk_customers.sql_formula: SELECT COUNT(*) AS at_risk_count FROM (SELECT customer_id, julianday('2011-12-10') - julianday(MAX(order_purchase_timestamp)) AS days_inactive FROM orders WHERE order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY customer_id) sub WHERE days_inactive BETWEEN 20 AND 30

churn_by_segment.label: Churn Rate by Customer Segment
churn_by_segment.definition: Churn rates per RFM segment: Champions, High-Value, Mid-Value, Low-Value. From model evaluation: Low-Value 87.2%, Mid-Value 76.4%, High-Value 52.9%, Champions 38.4%.

revenue_forecast_30d.label: Forecasted Revenue Next 30 Days
revenue_forecast_30d.definition: Prophet model prediction of total revenue for the next 30 days, including confidence interval.

revenue_growth_mom.label: Revenue Growth Month-over-Month
revenue_growth_mom.definition: Percentage change in monthly revenue compared to the previous month.
revenue_growth_mom.sql_formula: SELECT month, revenue, LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_revenue, ROUND((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100.0 / LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 2) AS mom_growth_pct FROM (SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', o.order_purchase_timestamp) AS month, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month)

order_growth_mom.label: Order Count Growth Month-over-Month
order_growth_mom.definition: Percentage change in number of orders compared to the previous month.
order_growth_mom.sql_formula: SELECT month, order_count, LAG(order_count) OVER (ORDER BY month) AS prev_count, ROUND((order_count - LAG(order_count) OVER (ORDER BY month)) * 100.0 / LAG(order_count) OVER (ORDER BY month), 2) AS growth_pct FROM (SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', order_purchase_timestamp) AS month, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders GROUP BY month ORDER BY month)

seller_revenue_concentration.label: Seller Revenue Concentration (Top 10%)
seller_revenue_concentration.definition: Percentage of total revenue generated by the top 10% of sellers. High concentration indicates over-reliance on a few sellers.
seller_revenue_concentration.sql_formula: SELECT ROUND(SUM(revenue) * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(oi2.price + oi2.freight_value) FROM order_items oi2 JOIN orders o2 ON oi2.order_id = o2.order_id WHERE o2.order_status = 'delivered'), 2) AS top10pct_revenue_share FROM (SELECT oi.seller_id, SUM(oi.price + oi.freight_value) AS revenue FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY oi.seller_id ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT (SELECT CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT seller_id) * 0.1 AS INTEGER) FROM order_items))

items_per_category_per_order.label: Average Items per Category per Order
items_per_category_per_order.definition: How many items from a specific category customers typically buy in one order. Useful for bundle recommendations.
items_per_category_per_order.sql_formula: SELECT t.product_category_name_english AS category, ROUND(COUNT(oi.order_item_id) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT oi.order_id), 2) AS avg_items FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id JOIN product_category_name_translation t ON p.product_category_name = t.product_category_name GROUP BY category ORDER BY avg_items DESC LIMIT 10
